L1.area: Mathematical data types and arithmetic operations

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists due to a misalignment in range validation between integer and decimal numeric types. The maximum allowed bit-length for decimal values is computed to be larger than the maximum for integer values, creating an inconsistency. This allows decimal values to be constructed that appear valid under decimal range checks but exceed the representable range of the integer type. When such an oversized decimal interacts with integer operations, it causes an unrecoverable panic, leading to denial of service. An attacker can trigger this through any input path that creates or deserializes decimal values exceeding the intended bounds.

L3.area: Decimal type arithmetic and range validation in the SDK math library
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the Cosmos SDK math library due to a misalignment in range validation between integer and legacy decimal numeric types. The maximum allowed bit-length for decimal values is computed to be larger than the maximum for integer values. This allows decimal values to be constructed through string parsing, arithmetic operations, or deserialization that appear valid under decimal range checks but exceed the representable range of the integer type. When such an oversized decimal interacts with integer operations, it causes an unrecoverable panic, leading to denial of service.

ground_truth.reason: The maxDecBitLen constant used for decimal range validation was larger than MaxBitLen used for integers (315 bits vs 256 bits), allowing decimal values that exceed the integer type's valid range and causing panics when interacting with integer operations.
